perturbation.original_text: (b) During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity other than solely as a salesman or as a sales representative) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "other than solely as a salesman or as a sales representative", the clause now contradicts the exception provided previously in 2(a) (before the first perturbation was made), which allowed Principal to be employed as a salesman or sales representative. The change means Principal's involvement in any capacity (other than as an investor within the prescribed limits) in a competing enterprise is now restricted, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exception "other than solely as a salesman or as a sales representative" from the original text, which now contradicts the clause in the contradicted text. The contradicted text now implies that Principal cannot be involved in the brown shoe market segment in any capacity, directly or indirectly. Thus, the change introduces a clear contradiction because it eliminates a previously allowed exception, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The covenants set forth in this Section 2 shall be construed as a series of separate covenants covering their subject matter in each of the separate states within the -2- <PAGE> Territory and, except for geographic coverage, each such separate covenant shall be deemed identical in terms to the covenant set forth above in this Section 2. To the extent that any such covenant shall be judicially unenforceable in any one or more of such states, such covenant shall not be affected with respect to each of the other states in the Territory. Each covenant with respect to each such state in the Territory shall be construed as severable and independent.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants set forth in this Section 2 shall be construed as a series of separate covenants covering their subject matter in each of the separate states within the -2- <PAGE> Territory. To the extent that any such covenant shall be judicially unenforceable in any one or more of such states, such covenant shall not be affected with respect to each of the other states in the Territory. Each covenant with respect to each such state in the Territory shall be construed as severable and independent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and, except for geographic coverage, each such separate covenant shall be deemed identical in terms to the covenant set forth above in this Section 2", the text now becomes contradictory since each state may interpret the terms differently. This phrase ensured uniformity in the application of the covenants across all states except for the geographical scope. Now, without it, the interpretation of the covenants could vary from state to state, creating uncertainty and potential contradiction in their application.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The covenants set forth in this Section 2 shall be construed as a series of separate covenants covering their subject matter in each of the separate states within the -2- <PAGE> Territory. To the extent that any such covenant shall be judicially unenforceable in any one or more of such states, such covenant shall not be affected with respect to each of the other states in the Territory. Each covenant with respect to each such state in the Territory shall be construed as severable and independent.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured uniformity in the application of covenants across states except for geographical scope. The changed text removes this uniformity, implying that each state could interpret the covenants differently. This directly contradicts the unchanged text, which expects covenants to be construed as 'separate' but not explicitly 'identical'. Without the 'identical' clause, the interpretation of the covenants becomes inconsistent across states creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. If this Section 2 shall for any reason be held excessively broad as to time, duration, geographical scope, activity or subject, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. If this Section 2 shall for any reason be held excessively broad as to time, duration, activity or subject, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "geographical scope" from the clause 'If this Section 2 shall for any reason be held excessively broad as to time, duration, geographical scope, activity or subject, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws,' the agreement states that all terms will be compatible with laws. But by removing this phrase, If a geographical scope is found to be too broad, it would be completely unenforceable, contradicting the initial acknowledgement that covenants are integral.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. If this Section 2 shall for any reason be held excessively broad as to time, duration, activity or subject, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, acknowledging reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area, and scope. It also states that if the section is excessively broad, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws. The changed text removes 'geographical scope' from the clause allowing for enforceability compatible with applicable laws if the section is excessively broad. This creates a contradiction because if the geographical scope is found to be too broad, it would be completely unenforceable, contradicting the initial acknowledgement that covenants are integral. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, and thus highlights that the original section was designed to be integral, which conflicts with the changes.

perturbation.original_text: (a) The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans. The parties hereto agree that the failure of any Confidential Information to be marked or otherwise labeled as confidential or proprietary information shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The parties hereto agree that the failure of any Confidential Information to be marked or otherwise labeled as confidential or proprietary information shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.' the clause regarding confidential information now implies that if information is not marked as confidential, it is not considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Confidential Information does not need to be marked as such to be considered confidential. The changed text removes this statement, implying that unmarked information may not be considered confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is the same as the original text excerpt, but that doesn't matter), which defines Confidential Information without requiring it to be marked, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Principal acknowledges and agrees that Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition will suffer irreparable harm in the event that Principal breaches any of its obligations under this Agreement, and that monetary damages shall be inadequate to compensate Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition for any such breach. Principal agrees that in the event of any breach or threatened breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction in order to prevent or restrain any such breach or threatened breach by Principal, or by any or all of Principal's agents, representatives or other persons directly or indirectly acting for, on behalf of, or with Principal.
perturbation.changed_text: Principal acknowledges and agrees that Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition will suffer irreparable harm in the event that Principal breaches any of its obligations under this Agreement, and that monetary damages shall be inadequate to compensate Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition for any such breach. Principal agrees that in the event of any breach or threatened breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, in order to prevent or restrain any such breach or threatened breach by Principal, or by any or all of Principal's agents, representatives or other persons directly or indirectly acting for, on behalf of, or with Principal.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'permanent injunction' from the sentence 'Principal agrees that in the event of any breach or threatened breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction', it limits the remedies available, suggesting that harm is not lasting. This contradicts the acknowledgement of irreparable harm in the initial statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Principal acknowledges and agrees that Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition will suffer irreparable harm in the event that Principal breaches any of its obligations under this Agreement, and that monetary damages shall be inadequate to compensate Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition for any such breach. Principal agrees that in the event of any breach or threatened breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, in order to prevent or restrain any such breach or threatened breach by Principal, or by any or all of Principal's agents, representatives or other persons directly or indirectly acting for, on behalf of, or with Principal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'permanent injunction' from the remedies available to Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition in the event of a breach by Principal. The original text states that Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would suffer 'irreparable harm' and monetary damages would be inadequate. Removing the possibility of a permanent injunction suggests that the harm may not be irreparable or that other remedies are sufficient, directly contradicting the acknowledgement of irreparable harm stated in both the original text and the contradicted text. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
